632: export async function processWorkspaceTask(env: Env, workspaceId: string, taskId: string): Promise<void> {
633:   const claimed = await claimWorkspaceTaskForExecution(env.DB, workspaceId, taskId);
634:   if (!claimed) {
635:     const existing = await getWorkspaceTask(env.DB, workspaceId, taskId);
636:     if (existing?.status === 'running') {
637:       throw new QueueRetryError('Workspace task is already running; defer redelivery');
638:     }
639:     return;
640:   }
641:   let task: WorkspaceTaskResponse | null = null;
642:   try {
643:     const flags = await loadRuntimeFlags(env);
644:     if (!flags.workspaceAgentRuntimeEnabled) {
645:       await updateWorkspaceTaskStatus(env.DB, taskId, 'failed', {
646:         workspaceId,

650:       await appendWorkspaceTaskEvent(env.DB, {

652:         taskId,
653:         eventType: 'task_failed',
654:         payload: {

656:           message: 'Workspace agent runtime is disabled',
657:         },
658:       });
659:       return;
660:     }
661: 
662:     task = await getWorkspaceTask(env.DB, workspaceId, taskId);
663:     if (!task) {

665:     }
666: 
667:     const payload = await getWorkspaceTaskRequestPayload(env.DB, taskId);
668:     const toolPolicyInput = await getWorkspaceTaskToolPolicy(env.DB, workspaceId, taskId);
669:     if (!payload) {

671:         workspaceId,
672:         errorCode: 'task_not_found',
673:         errorMessage: 'Workspace task payload no longer exists',
674:       });
675:       return;
676:     }
677: 
678:     const toolPolicy = normalizeToolPolicy(toolPolicyInput);
679:     await executeWorkspaceTask(env, workspaceId, task, payload, toolPolicy);
680:   } catch (error) {
681:     const freshTask = await getWorkspaceTask(env.DB, workspaceId, taskId);
682:     const attemptCount = freshTask?.attemptCount ?? task?.attemptCount ?? 0;
683:     const maxRetries = freshTask?.maxRetries ?? task?.maxRetries ?? 0;
684: 
685:     if (freshTask?.cancelRequestedAt) {
686:       await updateWorkspaceTaskStatus(env.DB, taskId, 'cancelled', {
687:         workspaceId,
688:         result: { reason: 'cancel_requested' },
689:         errorCode: null,
690:         errorMessage: null,
691:       });
692:       await appendWorkspaceTaskEvent(env.DB, {
693:         workspaceId,
694:         taskId,
695:         eventType: 'task_cancelled',
696:         payload: { reason: 'cancel_requested' },
697:       });
698:       return;
699:     }
700: 
701:     if (error instanceof PolicyError) {
702:       await updateWorkspaceTaskStatus(env.DB, taskId, 'failed', {
703:         workspaceId,
704:         errorCode: error.code,
705:         errorMessage: error.message,
706:       });
707:       await appendWorkspaceTaskEvent(env.DB, {
708:         workspaceId,
709:         taskId,
710:         eventType: 'task_failed',
711:         payload: { code: error.code, message: error.message },
712:       });
713:       return;
714:     }
715: 
716:     if ((error instanceof QueueRetryError || isTransientFailure(error)) && attemptCount <= maxRetries) {
717:       await updateWorkspaceTaskStatus(env.DB, taskId, 'queued', {
718:         workspaceId,
719:         startedAt: null,
720:         finishedAt: null,
721:         errorCode: 'retry_scheduled',

723:       });
724:       await appendWorkspaceTaskEvent(env.DB, {
725:         workspaceId,

731:         },
732:       });

734:     }
735: 
736:     const message = sanitizeErrorMessage(error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error));
737:     await updateWorkspaceTaskStatus(env.DB, taskId, 'failed', {
738:       workspaceId,
739:       errorCode: 'task_execution_failed',
740:       errorMessage: message,
741:     });

743:       workspaceId,
744:       taskId,
745:       eventType: 'task_failed',

749:       },

parts.text:
I reviewed the current workspace-task runtime changes across the Worker API, DB layer, queue handling, runner, migration, and related tests.

No blocking defects stood out in the implementation itself. The flow is coherent end-to-end (create → enqueue/execute → events/status → cancel/retry), and the policy/idempotency handling looks internally consistent.

- I did not find a clear correctness/security bug worth flagging as a must-fix before merge.
- I could not execute the test suite in this environment because the local runtime is Node `v14.16.0` while the repo requires Node `>=20` (pnpm engine check fails), so this review is static-analysis only.
- If you want, I can do a second pass focused only on failure-mode behavior (queue redelivery, idempotency races, cancellation during tool execution) once tests can run under Node 20+.
